problem: How do we prevent stolen refresh tokens from being used indefinitely?
decision: Refresh tokens rotate on every use; server keeps a one-generation family id and revokes the whole family on replay.
alternatives: Long-lived non-rotating refresh token with IP pinning
No refresh token — re-auth every session
constraints: Refresh endpoint is idempotent within a short grace window to tolerate retries
Builds on d-2026-01-10-auth-jwt-in-cookie-001 cookie storage assumption
failures: Attacker who steals a refresh token keeps refreshing forever
expiry_conditions: We switch to opaque server-side sessions (refresh rotation becomes unnecessary)

future_reminders.who_might_repeat: engineer investigating an auth incident or hardening flow
future_reminders.revisit_when: session model changes from stateless JWT to server-side
